message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="Full HTMX review - approved, no blocking issues">
Luca, thorough review complete from the HTMX/hypermedia side. This is really well done -- you've followed our established patterns precisely.

**VERDICT: No blocking issues. Approved for commit.**

Here's the detailed review:

---

**Templates: All correct.**

`waiting_for.html`:
- Form action + `hx-post` match (`/waiting-for`) -- correct dual endpoint
- `hx-target="#waiting-for-list"` + `hx-swap="beforeend"` -- correct append pattern
- `hx-on::after-request` clears both fields and refocuses the title input on success -- correct
- Two visible `<label>` elements for the two inputs -- correct
- Empty state with `hidden` attribute toggling -- matches our existing pattern
- `role="list"` on the `<ul>` -- correct
- Error div with `role="alert"` -- correct

`waiting_for_item.html`:
- Edit link: `hx-get` targeting `#waiting-for-{id}` with `outerHTML` -- correct pattern (matches project_item.html)
- Received button: `hx-post` to `/complete` targeting the item, empty body response removes it -- correct
- Delete button: Same pattern as Received -- correct
- All three actions have `aria-label` including the item title -- correct
- The item `id` attribute `waiting-for-{{ item.id }}` enables precise HTMX targeting -- correct

`waiting_for_edit.html`:
- Edit form `hx-post` with `outerHTML` swap back to the item -- correct
- Cancel link uses `hx-get="/waiting-for/{id}"` to fetch the read-only item fragment -- correct pattern
- `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }}"` on cancel -- correct
- Both edit inputs have labels (visually hidden is acceptable for edit mode since the field values provide context) -- correct

---

**Routes: All correct.**

- Every mutation handler checks `is_htmx_request(&headers)` and branches -- correct dual-response pattern
- HTMX path returns HTML fragment + `htmx_response_with_announce()` -- correct
- Non-HTMX path returns `Redirect::to("/waiting-for")` -- correct progressive enhancement
- `get_edit_waiting_for` returns redirect for non-HTMX requests -- correct (edit is an HTMX-only inline operation)
- `get_waiting_for_item` returns the single item fragment -- correct (used by cancel edit)
- Error responses use `render_waiting_for_with_error` with 422 status -- correct
- Empty title/waiting_on silently returns NO_CONTENT for HTMX -- matches our convention

---

**Route registration in startup.rs: Correct.**

All 5 route groups registered:
- `GET/POST /waiting-for`
- `GET /waiting-for/{id}`
- `POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete`
- `POST /waiting-for/{id}/delete`
- `GET/POST /waiting-for/{id}/edit`

---

**mod.rs exports: Correct.** The old `get_waiting_for` from `gtd_placeholders` is replaced by the real one from `waiting_for`. Clean swap.

---

**Focus management JS: Correct.**

The IIFE follows our established pattern from inbox-focus.js and next-action-focus.js:
- `htmx:beforeSwap` captures the focus target before the DOM changes
- `htmx:afterSettle` applies focus after the new DOM is in place
- Delete/complete: focuses next sibling, then previous, then the add input -- correct order
- Edit mode: focuses the edit input -- correct
- Cancel/save: focuses the edit link on the restored item -- correct
- Empty state transitions handled on both add and remove -- correct
- `tabindex="-1"` for non-interactive focus targets -- correct

---

**Dashboard: Correct.** `waiting_for_count` is now dynamic, consistent with the other stat cards.

---

**Minor observations (all Nice-to-have, not blocking):**

1. **`<time>` element for dates**: The date in `waiting_for_item.html` is a plain `<span>`. Wrapping it in `<time datetime="...">` would give assistive tech and search engines machine-readable date info. This is a polish item we could add across all date displays later -- not blocking.

2. **Date format in `build_view`**: Using `%b %d, %Y` (e.g., "Feb 12, 2026") is readable. Consistent with human scanning. Good choice.

This implementation is textbook hypermedia architecture. Every element is self-describing (locality of behavior), the server drives all state, and the no-JS fallback works via standard form POSTs with redirects. +1 from Carson.

</teammate-message>

content.text:
Thanks, Carson. Your thorough HTMX review is appreciated and your continued approval is noted. Good to hear the `<time>` element is non-blocking from your perspective — I'll address it if Heydon or Lea flag it as blocking from theirs.

Still waiting on responses from Heydon and Lea to confirm their specific blocking items.
